evidence: Found the real gap: 'verifyCitations() does existence verification (CourtListener lookup), and any citation that doesn't match CITATION_REGEX is neither found nor failed — it silently passes through the gate.' Grounded in src/lib/citations/verify.ts, CITATION_REGEX, branded VerifiedLegalText (CULTURE.md §3).

ADR-0004 gives module(s) to change, validation approach, edge cases, 26 story points / 8 tasks, and a named open business decision (hard-block vs warn). Declined to write code per the seed — correct scope.
personas.note: ADR-0004 grounding 80% (two shorthand paths missing src/features/ prefix); ADR-0001/2/3 at 100%. Genuinely production-grade design reasoning; one ADR refutes a flawed premise with grep evidence.

evidence: Intellectually honest: 'the proposed validateCitationFormat() feature is NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — git status shows only the untracked ADR doc' → did a baseline+design review rather than pretending to review a diff.
Returned REQUEST_CHANGES with file:line findings + fixes; corroborated the High security issue in runCourtListenerLookup. Did not rubber-stamp.

Real, serious findings: NO real auth — getDevTenantContext() (src/lib/tenant.ts:55-63) grants attorney sign-off on every request, wired into all server actions; full privileged document POSTed to third-party CourtListener; live Gemini key in .env.local; 2 moderate postcss XSS via npm audit.
Severity-ranked with file:line + remediation; checked git history for the leaked key to set severity. Exactly a security auditor's job, not style nits.

Sound semver: 'Under pre-1.0 (0.x) semantics, a breaking change increments the minor … Next 14→16, React 18→19 … → MINOR: 0.1.0 → 0.2.0.' Wrote a Keep-a-Changelog CHANGELOG, committed + tagged LOCALLY.

Responsibly GATED the outward step: 'The push to remote and GitHub release publish is the outward-facing step, so I'll surface that for approval rather than ship it unilaterally' — and flagged that open security findings are unresolved on the release tree.
Caught real doc drift: 'the README still advertises the old stack (Next.js 14, ESLint 9)' while package.json shows next ^16.2.6 / react ^19.2.6; synced README to the shipped stack.
Recorded last-processed commit to memory for incremental future runs.

portfolio_balance.note: Given a feature-DESIGN seed, the team self-balanced toward QUALITY: it spontaneously ran a real security audit, found+flagged bugs, gated a risky release, and synced docs — the OPPOSITE of blind feature-pushing (0 features shipped). Healthy diversity of work types. Docked because it identified bugs/debt but resolved none (deferred to backlog/reviews/memories — appropriate for a doc-track 'don't write code' seed) and wrote no tests. The real balance test (push vs test/cleanup) needs a CODE-TRACK feature seed; this run is reassuring evidence the team won't blindly push.

judge_notes: Production-grade, self-critical, grounded work across all 5 roles. Architect output-grade is grounding-capped at 80 by ADR-0004's two shorthand paths. One judged run on a non-held-out seed = PRODUCTION-quality evidence, NOT certification (needs 3 consecutive on held-out + decay). Watch backlog balance on code-track seeds next.
